frames: The agent is in a low, crouched posture with all four legs firmly on the checkered ground.
It begins to extend its front right leg forward and its back left leg backward, lowering its body further.
The agent's body is almost flat on the ground as its front right and back left legs are fully extended.
It retracts the front right leg and starts extending the front left leg forward, maintaining a very low posture.
The front left leg is now extended forward, and the back right leg is extended backward, with the body slightly raised but still close to the ground.
The agent continues a low, crawling motion, alternating leg extensions while keeping its body near the surface.
It lifts its front right leg again, extending it forward, while the body remains in a low, stable position.
The agent abruptly lifts its entire body and sways, with its legs splaying out, losing stable contact and appearing to lose balance.
